reason: claudecode's Task/Agent tool is foreground-by-default: it blocks the parent's turn until the subagent reports back. There is no current user-facing SendMessage-to-background-child primitive in the claude CLI that would produce the scenario's defining shape (parent transitions to ready while a named child continues working). Additionally, irrlicht's metrics layer does not currently distinguish background from foreground children in SubagentCount(parent). When/if Anthropic ships a true background-task primitive analogous to OpenCode's run_in_background, the existing file-based discovery path would already pick up the child transcript — the agent-level gap is upstream, not in irrlicht.
notes: Spec preserved for forward compatibility: parent working→ready at turn end while child stays alive; child state unchanged by parent's transition; SubagentCount(parent) excludes the background child; background child disappears within readyTTL after it finishes. Future phases (when capability lands): parent_session_birth (ready), parent_turn_start (working), background_child_birth (transcript_new), parent_turn_end (ready while child still alive), background_child_end (process_exited or transcript_removed on child only, no parent transition).
